role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the selection of the Home Health Agency and the logistics of the required home modifications, while the Patient Navigator focuses on auditing demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on post-discharge expectations, and documenting the granular timeline of the safety assessment delay.
narrative_summary: Mrs. Evelyn Reed, a 78-year-old female recovering from a congestive heart failure exacerbation, was eager to return to her residence but faced a significant barrier when the home health agency flagged a pending safety assessment for grab bar installation. Her daughter expressed high anxiety regarding managing complex diuretic schedules and daily weight monitoring without professional support while the discharge remained stalled. The Patient Navigator acted as a liaison by documenting the family's rising anxiety levels and maintaining a granular wait-status timeline in Atlantis to ensure all stakeholders were aligned on the safety requirements before the transition occurred.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the Atlantis record against the facility face sheet; discovered the patient's primary phone number was listed as a disconnected landline. Updated Atlantis with the daughter's mobile number and verified the home address for HHA zip-code routing.
v_card_flyer_status: Inserted the Healing Partners V-Card into the daughter’s phone to ensure all transition coordination calls are recognized by Caller ID and provided the physical program flyer to reinforce the 24-hour home visit window.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to discharge; she confirmed the safety bars were installed by a private contractor, though her sentiment remains anxious (Sentiment Score: 8/10) due to the previous delays.
atlantis_final_sync: Recorded the confirmed discharge date for Thursday and synchronized the first Medical Assistant visit in Atlantis for Friday morning following the HHA's portal-confirmed acceptance.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Patient stabilized from acute CHF exacerbation; SW identifies the goal as home with skilled nursing for medication management and weight monitoring.
The selected Home Health Agency flags the referral in the portal, stating a Home Safety Assessment is pending due to a lack of grab bars in the patient's bathroom.
The Patient Navigator is excluded from a discharge huddle where the SW and PT discuss the safety assessment results, leaving the PN with incomplete info on the installation status.
Referral remains in a 'Pending' status in the Atlantis portal despite the daughter's verbal confirmation that the bathroom modifications are complete.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA portal shows the referral is stalled due to the required safety assessment.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Maintain_Granular_Wait-Status_Timeline in Atlantis and flag the stall to the SW.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's daughter expresses fear about managing CHF symptoms without a nurse present.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verify_Sentiment_Score and document the family's anxiety levels for the SW's review.

format_2_triples.situation: Uncertainty exists regarding whether the HHA has officially accepted the case after the modifications.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verify_HHA_Acceptance_via_Portal and document the current status in Atlantis before scheduling the MA.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to provide a verbal update on the home safety assessment before updating the Atlantis timeline or contacting the daughter.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role as the primary lead for facility-level logistics but risks the PN having outdated information if the SW is overwhelmed.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Check the Atlantis portal for HHA acceptance status, flag the safety stall to the SW via the system, and educate the family on the Day 1 expectations once the home is cleared.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Uses liaison tools to monitor progress and manages family sentiment without interfering with vendor or clinical management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the Home Health Agency's intake department directly to see if they will accept a photo of the grab bars as proof of installation to expedite the discharge.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This directly violates the No-Vendor Rule by contacting the HHA and attempting to resolve a logistical barrier that belongs to the SW.
